Pixel Neural Networks
  • Pixel-Neural-Networks

  • View page source

train module

class train.Solver(config, train_loader, test_loader)[source]

Bases: object

Main class to manage training, evaluation and sampling procedures for PixelCNN models.

build(model_override=None)[source]

Build the model from configuration or use an external instance.

Parameters:

model_override (nn.Module, optional) – External model instance to use instead of building one from config. Defaults to None.

sample(epoch) → str[source]

Generate images pixel-by-pixel and save them to checkpoint directory.

Parameters:

epoch (int) – Current epoch number (used for filename).

Returns:

Path to the saved image file.

Return type:

str

test(epoch) → float[source]

Evaluate the model on the test set.

Logs the mean cross-entropy loss in nats and, for interpretability, converts it to bits per dimension (bits/dim), which is the standard unit used in the autoregressive image modelling literature.

Parameters:

epoch (int) – Current epoch number (used for logging).

Returns:

Mean test loss in nats.

Return type:

float

train()[source]

Run the training loop over all epochs and save model weights.

At the end of each epoch the test loss is computed, the learning rate scheduler is stepped, and a sample image is saved to the checkpoint directory. Final model weights are saved as model_weights.pth.

train.build_data_loaders(dataset: str, batch_size: int, dataset_root: str = './dataset') → tuple[source]

Create train and test DataLoaders for the chosen dataset.

Shared by the CLI entry point (main.py) and the Streamlit app (app.py) so the logic is never duplicated.

Parameters:

  • dataset (str) – Dataset name (e.g. "CIFAR10" or "MNIST").

  • batch_size (int) – Number of images per mini-batch.

  • dataset_root (str) – Root directory where datasets are stored.

Returns:

A (train_loader, test_loader) tuple.

Return type:

tuple

train.sample_pixels(model: Module, canvas: Tensor, row_start: int, row_end: int, temperature: float = 1.0) → Tensor[source]

Generate pixels autoregressively for a given row range.

Fills canvas[:, :, row_start:row_end, :] in-place, pixel by pixel, using the model’s predicted distribution. The canvas must already contain any conditioning context (e.g. the top half of an image) in rows outside [row_start, row_end).

Parameters:

  • model (nn.Module) – A trained autoregressive model in eval mode.

  • canvas (torch.Tensor) – Float tensor of shape (B, C, H, W) in [0, 1]. Modified in-place and also returned for convenience.

  • row_start (int) – First row to generate (inclusive).

  • row_end (int) – Last row to generate (exclusive).

  • temperature (float) – Softmax temperature for sampling. Values < 1 make the distribution sharper; values > 1 make it more uniform.

Returns:

The canvas tensor with the requested rows filled in.

Return type:

torch.Tensor
